ddls:
CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for login */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for login */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user-role mapping */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "data_source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "source_description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  "source_url" TEXT /* URL of the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "policy_responses" (
  "policy_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each policy response */,
  "case_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the data entry (economic indicator) */,
  "policy_name" TEXT /* Name of the policy */,
  "policy_description" TEXT /* Description of the policy */,
  "date_issued" TEXT /* Date the policy was issued */,
  "date_ended" TEXT /* Date the policy ended */,
  "impact_rating" TEXT /* Impact rating of the policy (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("policy_id")
)
column_names: Unique identifier for each user-role mapping
Unique identifier for each data source
Unique identifier for each policy response
Identifier for the data entry (economic indicator)
Impact rating of the policy (e.g., high, medium, low)
